english: standing at; to the Tibetans asked by us the word seemed to be unknown, and the MS. of Kyelang has *ri-ba* (ri ba) in the above cited passage.
*rib-ma* (rib ma) W., *ḍib-ma* (dib ma) C., fence, hedge, enclosure to protect the fields from cold winds, intruders etc.

*rim-gro* (rim gro) or *rim-_gro* (rim 'gro), resp. *sku-rim* (sku rim) honour, homage, shown more esp. to gods, saints, and priests, offerings and other ceremonies (v. sub *sku* (sku)), *rim-gros tar-bar _gyur* (rim gros thar bar 'gyur) he will yet be cured by religious ceremonies (if medical advise should prove insufficient) S.g.; *dei rim-gro-la* (de'i rim gro la) as a ceremony for him (the sick person) Mil.; *zaṅ-ziṅ-gi rim-gros* (zang zing gi rim gros) by offerings in goods, cattle etc.) Mil.; *rim-_gro cén-po byas* (rim 'gro chen po byas) he arranged a great sacrificial festival Pth.; *rim-gro-pa* (rim gro pa) servant, waiting-man, valet de chambre.

1. series, succession, *rim-(pa) bźin(-du)* (rim (pa) bzhin (du)) Dzl., *rim-par* (rim par) Glr., in a row or line, in rows, by turns, successively, one after another, also = by degrees, gradually; *rim-gyis* (rim gyis), *rim-pas* (rim pas) Dzl. id.; *rim-pas daṅ-po mčog yin pyi-ma dman* (rim pas dang po mchog yin phyi ma dman) v. sub *dman-pa* (dman pa); *bya-ba łob-rim bźin byed-pa* (bya ba lhob rim bzhin byed pa) to do a business by turns, each taking a certain share of the work Glr. — 2. the place in a row or file, constituent part or member of a series, *dei mi-brgyud rim-pa lṅas rgyal-sa bzuṅ* (de'i mi brgyud rim pa lngas rgyal sa bzung) five members of his lineage occupied the throne Glr., and in a still more general sense: *sgo rim-pa bdun* (sgo rim pa bdun) a sevenfold door Dzl.; *rim-ldabs* (rim ldabs) Sch. and *ñis-rim* (nyis rim) S.g. double; *rim-gcig* (rim gcig) = *lan-gcig* (lan gcig) one time, once. — 3. order, method, *°cád-par _gyúr-baí rim-pa* (bcad par 'gyur ba'i rim pa) *dis* ('dis) by this method which will be explained immediately, Sch.; *rim-bral* (rim bral) disorderly, irregular Cs. — *rim gñis* (rim gnyis) v. *skyed-rim* (bskyed rim).

contagious disease, epidemy, plague, *ñan-rims* (nyan rims) id. Glr.; *rims gtoṅ-ba* (rims gtong ba) to send, to cause a plague, as demons do Dzl.; *dus gdoṅ gñis-kyis ma skyed rims mi _byuṅ* (dus gdong gnyis kyis ma bskyed rims mi 'byung) plagues, epidemies, are caused by nothing but the season or by demons; *tu-rim* (tu rim) W. dysentery, diarrhoea, bloody flux; *rims-so* (rims so) the 'tooth' of an epi- demy, i.e. its contagium, virulency.

I. more frq. *ril-po* (ril po), *ril-mo* (ril mo) B., C.; *ril-ril* (ril ril) W. 1. round, globular, in C. also cylindrical; *sran-ma ril-mo* (sran ma ril mo) peas are round Wdn.; *ril-ril* (ril ril) W. also sbst.: a round, globular object, such as a cabbage-head, a round lump of butter etc.; *ril-bai spyi-blugs* (ril ba'i spyi blugs) Glr., Sch.: 'a bottle, narrow in the middle, a gourd-bottle'. — 2. whole, entire; wholly, quite *koṅ-ril* (kong ril) quite crippled, lamed C.; *nag-ril-ril* (nag ril ril) W. very black, quite black; *rtag-pa daṅ ril-por dzin-pa* (rtag pa dang ril por 'dzin pa) to consider a thing lasting and entire (not compounded) Thgy.; *ril-por ṅa dbaṅ-na* (ril por nga dbang na) if it belongs to me entirely Mil.; *ril-po* (ril po) the whole, the entire thing (opp. to a part), also in arithmetic Wdk.; *ril-poí lhag-ma* (ril po'i lhag ma) the remainder of the whole Wdk.; *bubs-ril lus* (bubs ril lus) the whole body S.g.; *ril-gyis gyogs-pa* (ril gyis g.yogs pa) entirely, completely, envelop- ed, or wrapped up Sch.; *ril-mid-pa* (ril mid pa) Sch.: 'to swallow a thing entire'; *de-dag daṅ ril-gyis mči-am pyed daṅ mči-ba bka-stsol cig* (de dag dang ril gyis mchi 'am phyed dang mchi ba bka' stsol cig) tell me whether I am to come with all, or only with one half (of them) Dzl. 282, 5 (acc. to the manuscript of Kyelang); *ril-bu* (ril bu), col. *ril-lu* (ril lu), small ball, globule, pill, *ril-bur bsgril-ba sran-ma tsam* (ril bur bsgril ba sran ma tsam) formed into a pill of the size of a pea Lt.; *ril-ma* (ril ma) globular dung of some animals, *byi-bai ril* (byi ba'i ril) mouse-dung Mng. (where Piper longum is compared with it), *gla-bai ril* (gla ba'i ril) dung of the musk-deer; *lug-ril* (lug ril) tirdles, sheep-pellets, *śa-ril* (sha ril) 1. dung of the argali Ld. 2. small meat-balls C. — II. 1. W.: *ril-če* (ril ce) (for *gril-ba* (gril ba)) to fall. — 2. Bal. *ril-cas* (ril cas) (for *sgril-ba* (sgril ba)) to wrap up.

1. cognate to *ri-mo* (ri mo) and perh. to *_bri-ba* ('bri ba): figure, form, design, *padma-ris* (padma ris) the figure of a lotus-flower Glr., *mig-maṅ-ris-su bris-pa* (mig mang ris su bris pa) Glr. painted like a chess- board; *skya-ris* (skya ris) the blank parts of a picture, *tson-ris* (tshon ris) the painted parts of a picture Cs. — 2. Cs.: part, region, quarter, hence *mto-ris* (mtho ris) heaven, v. *mto* (mtho); *dbaṅ-ris* (dbang ris) share of power or of territory; *mṅa-ris* (mnga' ris) id. and n. of a part of
